tweets.text: Glad to see the Common Cents Act passed the House. With our national debt over $36 trillion, we can’t keep spending taxpayer money on currency that costs more to make than it’s worth. This is a common-sense way to cut waste and get our fiscal house in order.

tweets.text: RT @JacobRobinsonJD: Should code ever lose First Amendment protections? This podcast covers free speech and software, including cryptography, cryptocurrency and AI. It should be the internet's most comprehensive. You'll hear from experts like @valkenburgh, @prestonjbyrne, @LazPieper, Sam Enzer, and Professors Eugene Volokh (@VolokhC) and @JaneYakowitz. We trace the history of Cold War export controls through to the recent restrictions on Anthropic's AI models, and explain why the Supreme Court has never definitively answered the question above. Timestamps: 0:00 Anthropic AI export controls 6:17 Intro 9:39 The First Amendment 10:38 @VolokhC on his free speech philosophy 13:42 Speech vs function 19:24 @valkenburgh interview 25:50 The Cold War, munitions list and Phil Zimmermann 29:57 The Bernstein case 34:53 Strict scrutiny 38:48 The Corley case 49:17 Sorrell, Stevens and where the Supreme Court is heading 53:25 @prestonjbyrne on the Supreme Court's AI case 57:23 Defense Distributed and 3D-printing 1:04:00 Where publication ends and conduct begins 1:15:20 Lowe v. SEC and the agency line 1:45:45 Compelled speech 2:04:17 @LazPieper on Sorrell and third-party liability 2:11:29 @prestonjbyrne on the GRANITE Act and foreign censorship Thank you to the presenting sponsor of this episode, @altitude. Nothing in this podcast is legal or investment advice.

tweets.text: RT @lordvolth: We often hear that TradFi is "skeptical" of blockchains and DeFi... in reality it's just discipline. These institutions aren't waiting to believe in the tech. They're just telling us a COGS story. @cc_crowley and I talk about the near-term vs. long-term opportunity in programmable financial infrastructure 👇 Capital Markets players are adopting exactly the primitives that improve unit economics: atomic settlement, programmable money, tokenized collateral. Decentralization, the part with no line item, maybe gets left behind? CLARITY would be a real step toward the "elegant convergence" everyone predicts. But there's a long window where the short game and the long game both matter. Not competing bets, but parallel ones. Near-term, builders can help institutions adopt the primitives that move more capital onchain. Long-term, an open system continues to evolve and push the innovations that may eventually subsume the old system. @a16zcrypto https://t.co/do9dblLknZ
tweets.summary: RT @lordvolth: We often hear that TradFi is "skeptical" of blockchains and DeFi... in reality it's just discipline. These institutions aren't waiting…

tweets.text: "The stablecoin requirements under Genius conflict with the requirements under MiCA, which is candidly nonsensical considering the whole point of crypto and stablecoins" Katherine on the contradiction between Genius and MiCA that's frustrating global crypto companies "The big difference here is they want to cover tokenized assets more generally, but they also want to cover stablecoins issued by non-EU stablecoin issuers. One source of frustration for anyone that works at a global crypto company is that the stablecoin requirements under Genius conflict with the requirements under MiCA" "You have a slew of stablecoin issuers with either a single issuance model, I believe Circle has single issuance, versus a multi-issuance model like MoonPay, which has multiple entities in different jurisdictions issuing different stables. What MiCA is now saying is, sorry US and non-EU stablecoin issuers, you have to get in line, you're going to have comparable standards to the EU issuers" "Everyone has a few months, till September I think, to provide input. So we won't see proposed regulatory revisions for at least a year probably. But what's happening in my mind is that Europe is kind of freaking out in response to the growth of tokenization and stables, and they're figuring out how to fix this"
tweets.summary: "The stablecoin requirements under Genius conflict with the requirements under MiCA, which is candidly nonsensical considering the whole point of…
